title: Startup founders urge Trump not to shut off Chinese open weight AI
excerpt: On Wednesday, the Little Tech Association sent letters to President Donald Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and others in the administration.
content_html:
Almost 200 Silicon Valley companies, including Proton and Y Combinator, are urging the Trump administration not to cut off access to Chinese open-weight artificial intelligence models or risk crippling the next generation of U.S. startups.

On Wednesday, the newly-formed Little Tech Association sent letters to President Donald Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and others in the administration with its appeal, marking the first coordinated effort by Silicon Valley's wider influential startup community to weigh in on one of the Trump administration's most closely watched AI debates. At issue: whether Washington should restrict access to increasingly powerful open-weight — meaning, AI models whose weights are publicly available — AI models released by Chinese companies such as Moonshot AI and Alibaba.

"American leadership requires two things: world-leading American open-weight models and continued access for U.S. builders to open models already available worldwide," the startup founders wrote in the letter obtained by POLITICO, also sent to Office of Science and Technology Policy Director Michael Kratsios. Instead of broad prohibitions, they argue the government should adopt targeted safeguards.

And they warn that banning Americans from downloading Chinese open-weight models wouldn't stop their proliferation — but would weaken U.S. startups.

"There'll be hundreds of companies that instantly die," said Suhail Doshi, founder of AI infrastructure startup Particle and a member of the association, which POLITICO first wrote about exclusively, in an interview. "It's great for Anthropic. We're all going to have to spend money on Anthropic."

The matter took on new urgency following reporting that the Trump administration was considering banning Chinese AI models, shortly after Moonshot AI unveiled its powerful Kimi K3 model.

"The United States leads the world in AI innovation, and President Trump will keep it that way. The Trump Administration is doubling down on innovation to widen the gap between America and the rest of the world," White House spokesperson Liz Huston said in a statement to West Wing Playbook.

A White House official granted anonymity to discuss the possibility of action against Chinese models said, "Any policy announcement will be announced directly by the administration. Any reporting until then is baseless speculation."

Those reports sparked panic across Silicon Valley's startup community, where many founders rely on the cheaper Chinese open-weight models to build products and lack the resources to spend heavily on usage credits from U.S. AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI. Among their fears: that banning these models would heavily advantage a handful of AI behemoths.

It also sparked discussions Monday night about the matter among senior White House and Cabinet members, according to two people familiar with the matter granted anonymity to speak about private discussions. But the idea of a blanket ban on Chinese open-weight models was not seriously discussed.

Still, administration officials have continued escalating their rhetoric against Chinese AI developers.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday on Fox Business' "Mornings with Maria" that the administration would investigate whether Chinese artificial intelligence companies had improperly distilled American models to power their own, saying that the U.S. can "sanction" companies that engage in intellectual property theft.

On Wednesday, Kratsios said the administration had information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic's Fable model while developing K3, alleging the Chinese company built "a sophisticated internal platform" to conduct large-scale distillation against American models while attempting to evade detection. Kratsios also alleged that Moonshot had acquired Nvidia GB300-equipped servers to train its models, despite a ban on their sale to Chinese entities.

Kratsios emphasized that the administration "strongly supports the free and fair development of AI, including a thriving competitive ecosystem that spans frontier models, specialized systems, open-source frameworks, and open-weight models," while drawing a distinction between legitimate model distillation and industrial-scale theft.

Moonshot has not publicly addressed the allegations.As of Wednesday, the Commerce Department — which maintains a list of companies subject to export controls and licensing restrictions, called the Entities List — had not drafted plans to include Chinese AI companies, according to the first person.

The group's position against banning open weight models also reflects a growing divide inside the AI industry, with heavyweights like Anthropic increasingly urging more restrictions on Chinese AI developers over security concerns, and startups arguing that bans would do little to help, but would put startups at a competitive disadvantage.

Little Tech Association Executive Director Harry Godfrey said in an interview that policymakers should use "a scalpel rather than a sledgehammer."

"The answer here would be: What is the lightest-touch way that doesn't raise costs, limit access or inhibit American innovation while still addressing" legitimate security concerns, he said.

This story originally appeared on POLITICO and is courtesy of the Axel Springer Global Reporters Network, which harnesses the resources of the company's newsrooms to publish ambitious scoops, investigations, interviews, opinion pieces, and analysis. It allows journalists — including those from POLITICO, Business Insider, WELT, BILD, Onet, and Fakt — to collaborate on major stories for an international audience of hundreds of millions across platforms.

sentences.zh: 特朗普政府正在加倍加大创新力度，以扩大美国与世界其他国家之间的差距，”白宫发言人莉兹·休斯顿在给《西翼剧本》的一份声明中表示。

blocks.html_en: A White House official granted anonymity to discuss the possibility of action against Chinese models said, "Any policy announcement will be announced directly by the administration. Any reporting until then is baseless speculation."

sentences.en: A White House official granted anonymity to discuss the possibility of action against Chinese models said, "Any policy announcement will be announced directly by the administration.
sentences.zh: 一位不愿透露姓名的白宫官员在讨论针对中国模式采取行动的可能性时表示，“任何政策公告都将由政府直接宣布。

sentences.en: Any reporting until then is baseless speculation."
sentences.zh: 在那之前的任何报道都是毫无根据的猜测。”

blocks.html_en: Those reports sparked panic across Silicon Valley's startup community, where many founders rely on the cheaper Chinese open-weight models to build products and lack the resources to spend heavily on usage credits from U.S. AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI. Among their fears: that banning these models would heavily advantage a handful of AI behemoths.

sentences.en: Those reports sparked panic across Silicon Valley's startup community, where many founders rely on the cheaper Chinese open-weight models to build products and lack the resources to spend heavily on usage credits from U.S. AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 这些报告引发了整个硅谷创业社区的恐慌，许多创始人依赖更便宜的中国开放权重模型来构建产品，并且缺乏资源来大量购买 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 等美国人工智能公司的使用积分。

sentences.en: Among their fears: that banning these models would heavily advantage a handful of AI behemoths.
sentences.zh: 他们担心的是：禁止这些模型将使少数人工智能巨头受益匪浅。

blocks.html_en: It also sparked discussions Monday night about the matter among senior White House and Cabinet members, according to two people familiar with the matter granted anonymity to speak about private discussions. But the idea of a blanket ban on Chinese open-weight models was not seriously discussed.

sentences.en: It also sparked discussions Monday night about the matter among senior White House and Cabinet members, according to two people familiar with the matter granted anonymity to speak about private discussions.
sentences.zh: 据两名不愿透露姓名的知情人士透露，这也引发了周一晚间白宫和内阁高级成员对此事的讨论。

sentences.en: But the idea of a blanket ban on Chinese open-weight models was not seriously discussed.
sentences.zh: 但全面禁止中国公开体重模型的想法并未得到认真讨论。

blocks.html_en: Still, administration officials have continued escalating their rhetoric against Chinese AI developers.

sentences.en: Still, administration officials have continued escalating their rhetoric against Chinese AI developers.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，政府官员仍在继续升级针对中国人工智能开发商的言论。

blocks.html_en: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday on Fox Business' "Mornings with Maria" that the administration would investigate whether Chinese artificial intelligence companies had improperly distilled American models to power their own, saying that the U.S. can "sanction" companies that engage in intellectual property theft.

sentences.en: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday on Fox Business' "Mornings with Maria" that the administration would investigate whether Chinese artificial intelligence companies had improperly distilled American models to power their own, saying that the U.S. can "sanction" companies that engage in intellectual property theft.
sentences.zh: 美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特周二在福克斯商业频道的“与玛丽亚共度早晨”节目中表示，政府将调查中国人工智能公司是否不当借鉴美国模型来为自己提供动力，并表示美国可以“制裁”从事知识产权盗窃的公司。

blocks.html_en: On Wednesday, Kratsios said the administration had information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic's Fable model while developing K3, alleging the Chinese company built "a sophisticated internal platform" to conduct large-scale distillation against American models while attempting to evade detection. Kratsios also alleged that Moonshot had acquired Nvidia GB300-equipped servers to train its models, despite a ban on their sale to Chinese entities.

sentences.en: On Wednesday, Kratsios said the administration had information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic's Fable model while developing K3, alleging the Chinese company built "a sophisticated internal platform" to conduct large-scale distillation against American models while attempting to evade detection.
sentences.zh: 周三，Kratsios 表示，政府获悉 Moonshot AI 在开发 K3 时提炼了 Anthropic 的 Fable 模型，并指控这家中国公司建立了“一个复杂的内部平台”，对美国模型进行大规模提炼，同时试图逃避检测。

sentences.en: Kratsios also alleged that Moonshot had acquired Nvidia GB300-equipped servers to train its models, despite a ban on their sale to Chinese entities.
sentences.zh: Kratsios 还声称，Moonshot 已经购买了配备 Nvidia GB300 的服务器来训练其模型，尽管这些服务器被禁止向中国实体出售。

blocks.html_en: Kratsios emphasized that the administration "strongly supports the free and fair development of AI, including a thriving competitive ecosystem that spans frontier models, specialized systems, open-source frameworks, and open-weight models," while drawing a distinction between legitimate model distillation and industrial-scale theft.

sentences.en: Kratsios emphasized that the administration "strongly supports the free and fair development of AI, including a thriving competitive ecosystem that spans frontier models, specialized systems, open-source frameworks, and open-weight models," while drawing a distinction between legitimate model distillation and industrial-scale theft.
sentences.zh: Kratsios 强调，政府“大力支持人工智能的自由和公平发展，包括涵盖前沿模型、专业系统、开源框架和开放权重模型的蓬勃发展的竞争生态系统”，同时区分合法模型蒸馏和工业规模盗窃。

blocks.html_en: Moonshot has not publicly addressed the allegations.As of Wednesday, the Commerce Department — which maintains a list of companies subject to export controls and licensing restrictions, called the Entities List — had not drafted plans to include Chinese AI companies, according to the first person.

sentences.en: Moonshot has not publicly addressed the allegations.As of Wednesday, the Commerce Department — which maintains a list of companies subject to export controls and licensing restrictions, called the Entities List — had not drafted plans to include Chinese AI companies, according to the first person.
sentences.zh: Moonshot 尚未公开回应这些指控。 据第一人士透露，截至周三，商务部尚未起草将中国人工智能公司纳入其中的计划。商务部负责维护一份受出口管制和许可限制的公司名单，称为“实体名单”。

blocks.html_en: The group's position against banning open weight models also reflects a growing divide inside the AI industry, with heavyweights like Anthropic increasingly urging more restrictions on Chinese AI developers over security concerns, and startups arguing that bans would do little to help, but would put startups at a competitive disadvantage.

sentences.en: The group's position against banning open weight models also reflects a growing divide inside the AI industry, with heavyweights like Anthropic increasingly urging more restrictions on Chinese AI developers over security concerns, and startups arguing that bans would do little to help, but would put startups at a competitive disadvantage.
sentences.zh: 该组织反对禁止开放权重模型的立场也反映了人工智能行业内部日益扩大的分歧，像 Anthropic 这样的重量级人物越来越多地敦促出于安全考虑对中国人工智能开发者施加更多限制，而初创公司则认为禁令无济于事，反而会使初创公司处于竞争劣势。

blocks.html_en: Little Tech Association Executive Director Harry Godfrey said in an interview that policymakers should use "a scalpel rather than a sledgehammer."

sentences.en: Little Tech Association Executive Director Harry Godfrey said in an interview that policymakers should use "a scalpel rather than a sledgehammer."
sentences.zh: 小科技协会执行董事哈里·戈弗雷在接受采访时表示，政策制定者应该使用“手术刀而不是大锤”。

blocks.html_en: "The answer here would be: What is the lightest-touch way that doesn't raise costs, limit access or inhibit American innovation while still addressing" legitimate security concerns, he said.

sentences.en: "The answer here would be: What is the lightest-touch way that doesn't raise costs, limit access or inhibit American innovation while still addressing" legitimate security concerns, he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，“这里的答案是：什么是最温和的方式，既不会增加成本、限制准入或抑制美国创新，同时仍然解决”合法的安全问题。

content_html_zh:
包括 Proton 和 Y Combinator 在内的近 200 家硅谷公司敦促特朗普政府不要切断对中国开放权重人工智能模型的访问，否则可能会导致下一代美国初创企业陷入瘫痪。

周三，新成立的小型科技协会向唐纳德·特朗普总统、商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克和其他政府官员发出了呼吁书，这标志着硅谷影响力更广泛的创业社区首次协调努力，参与特朗普政府最受关注的人工智能辩论之一。 争论的焦点是：华盛顿是否应该限制使用日益强大的开放权重——即权重公开的人工智能模型——由登月人工智能和阿里巴巴等中国公司发布的人工智能模型。

“美国的领导地位需要两件事：世界领先的美国开放重量模型，以及美国建筑商继续获得全球已有的开放模型，”这家初创公司的创始人在《政治》杂志获得的信中写道，这封信也寄给了科学技术政策办公室主任迈克尔·克拉齐奥斯(Michael Kratsios)。 他们认为政府不应采取广泛的禁令，而应采取有针对性的保障措施。

他们警告说，禁止美国人下载中国开放重量模型并不能阻止其扩散，反而会削弱美国初创企业的实力。

人工智能基础设施初创公司 Particle 的创始人、该协会成员 Suhail Doshi 表示：“将会有数百家公司立即消亡。”POLITICO 在接受采访时首次独家报道了该协会。 “这对 Anthropic 来说非常棒。 我们都得在 Anthropic 上花钱。”

在 Moonshot AI 推出强大的 Kimi K3 模型后不久，有报道称特朗普政府正在考虑禁止中国人工智能模型，此事变得更加紧迫。

“美国在人工智能创新方面处于世界领先地位，特朗普总统将保持这种状态。 特朗普政府正在加倍加大创新力度，以扩大美国与世界其他国家之间的差距，”白宫发言人莉兹·休斯顿在给《西翼剧本》的一份声明中表示。

一位不愿透露姓名的白宫官员在讨论针对中国模式采取行动的可能性时表示，“任何政策公告都将由政府直接宣布。 在那之前的任何报道都是毫无根据的猜测。”

这些报告引发了整个硅谷创业社区的恐慌，许多创始人依赖更便宜的中国开放权重模型来构建产品，并且缺乏资源来大量购买 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 等美国人工智能公司的使用积分。 他们担心的是：禁止这些模型将使少数人工智能巨头受益匪浅。

据两名不愿透露姓名的知情人士透露，这也引发了周一晚间白宫和内阁高级成员对此事的讨论。 但全面禁止中国公开体重模型的想法并未得到认真讨论。

尽管如此，政府官员仍在继续升级针对中国人工智能开发商的言论。

美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特周二在福克斯商业频道的“与玛丽亚共度早晨”节目中表示，政府将调查中国人工智能公司是否不当借鉴美国模型来为自己提供动力，并表示美国可以“制裁”从事知识产权盗窃的公司。

周三，Kratsios 表示，政府获悉 Moonshot AI 在开发 K3 时提炼了 Anthropic 的 Fable 模型，并指控这家中国公司建立了“一个复杂的内部平台”，对美国模型进行大规模提炼，同时试图逃避检测。 Kratsios 还声称，Moonshot 已经购买了配备 Nvidia GB300 的服务器来训练其模型，尽管这些服务器被禁止向中国实体出售。

Kratsios 强调，政府“大力支持人工智能的自由和公平发展，包括涵盖前沿模型、专业系统、开源框架和开放权重模型的蓬勃发展的竞争生态系统”，同时区分合法模型蒸馏和工业规模盗窃。

Moonshot 尚未公开回应这些指控。 据第一人士透露，截至周三，商务部尚未起草将中国人工智能公司纳入其中的计划。商务部负责维护一份受出口管制和许可限制的公司名单，称为“实体名单”。

该组织反对禁止开放权重模型的立场也反映了人工智能行业内部日益扩大的分歧，像 Anthropic 这样的重量级人物越来越多地敦促出于安全考虑对中国人工智能开发者施加更多限制，而初创公司则认为禁令无济于事，反而会使初创公司处于竞争劣势。

小科技协会执行董事哈里·戈弗雷在接受采访时表示，政策制定者应该使用“手术刀而不是大锤”。

他说，“这里的答案是：什么是最温和的方式，既不会增加成本、限制准入或抑制美国创新，同时仍然解决”合法的安全问题。

本文最初发表在 POLITICO 上，由阿克塞尔·施普林格全球记者网络提供，该网络利用公司新闻编辑室的资源来发布雄心勃勃的独家新闻、调查、采访、观点文章和分析。 它允许记者（包括来自 POLITICO、Business Insider、WELT、BILD、Onet 和 Fakt 的记者）跨平台合作为数亿国际受众撰写重大报道。
